title: Submit puck GNSS callbacks through unified location provider
description: lichen/apps/puck currently owns GNSS state directly in s_gps and sends LICHEN Native node_info from that app-local state. Migrate the GNSS callback to submit samples through the firmware-wide location provider, then have puck/native node_info consume provider snapshots. Acceptance: no-hardware tests or native_sim fakes cover GNSS no-fix/fix/stale behavior; physical GNSS validation remains separate.
notes: Implemented no-hardware puck GNSS provider slice. Code changes: added lichen/apps/puck/src/puck_location.{c,h}; puck GNSS callback now submits Zephyr GNSS samples through lichen_hal_location_submit() with ONBOARD_HARDWARE source, source_name gnss0, GNSS fix source, E7 lat/lon, meter + centimeter altitude, and clamped satellites. Periodic Native node_info now consumes lichen_hal_location_time_snapshot_get() and converts fresh provider snapshots into ln_gps_info; stale/no-fix snapshots omit Native GPS. Added optional altitude_cm_valid/altitude_cm to HAL sample/snapshot so Native keeps centimeter precision while existing altitude_m consumers remain unchanged; stale snapshots suppress both altitude forms. Added lichen/tests/puck_location native_sim/qemu harness covering null GNSS input, no-fix provider metadata without Native GPS, callback body -> provider -> Native GPS conversion with non-whole-meter altitude, and stale suppression. Added HAL tests for altitude_cm round-trip and stale suppression. Review chain: Newton found P1 callback was incorrectly gated by CONFIG_LICHEN_NATIVE and P2 Native altitude precision regression; Descartes found P1 altitude precision and P2 callback coverage gap. Fixed by gating callback only on LICHEN_HAL_GNSS_DEVICE, adding altitude_cm to HAL, preferring it for Native GPS, and exercising the callback wrapper in tests. Final re-reviews Aquinas and Mill reported no P0/P1/P2. Follow-up created: project-LICHEN-zte2.3.1 for pre-existing t1000_e/nrf52840 puck LR1110 loramac-node missing source build blocker. Local check: git diff --check PASS. EC2 validation on i-05e27e074db786e22 clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-location-v1: west twister -T lichen/tests/puck_location -T lichen/tests/hal -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-puck-location-v3 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS, 6/6 configs, 72 cases, 4 skipped, 0 warnings, 118.62s. RAK4631 puck app build: west build -p always -b rak4631/nrf52840 lichen/apps/puck -d build-puck-rak4631-location-v2 -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS; FLASH 99772 B / 1 MB (9.51%), RAM 34368 B / 256 KB (13.11%). T1000-E puck build attempted with west build -p always -b t1000_e/nrf52840 lichen/apps/puck -d build-puck-t1000-location-v1 -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen and failed before app link because missing modules/lib/loramac-node/src/radio/lr1110/lr1110_driver/src/lr1110_radio.c; tracked in project-LICHEN-zte2.3.1. Broad regression: west twister -T lichen/tests/puck_location -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/native_parse -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-puck-location-consumers-v2 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS, 8/9 configs passed, 1 static skip, 103 cases executed, 20 skipped, 0 warnings, 157.90s.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware puck GNSS provider integration with native_sim/qemu tests, RAK4631 app build, final clean code reviews, and T1000-E LR1110 build blocker filed as project-LICHEN-zte2.3.1.
